excerpts.text: I threw my gold medals and my diploma into the sea. They were nothing to me now. I had found the pearl of great price.
They put me in an insane asylum for 193 days because I preached the gospel too fervently at Union Theological Seminary. In that asylum, I read the Bible through forty times.
I went to America to study science. I earned my doctorate in chemistry. But God did not send me to America for chemistry. He sent me for the Cross.
Repent! Repent! Repent! This is my message to you. Without repentance there is no salvation. Without the Cross there is no hope.
I have preached in every province of China. I have seen tens of thousands turn to Christ. But the harvest is still not gathered. The laborers are still too few.
Sin must be named. The preacher who does not name sin is not a preacher — he is a flatterer.
At Union Seminary they taught me to doubt the Bible. They called the miracles myths. They called the resurrection a legend. I nearly lost my soul in that place.
I carry a coffin with me wherever I preach. It reminds me and my hearers that we must die to self before we can live for Christ.
excerpts.source_title: Account of revival meetings, recorded by associates

excerpts.text: God called me to be an evangelist, not a professor. I turned down a lucrative position in chemistry and chose the way of the Cross.
In Manila, two thousand people came forward. In Singapore, the revival continued for weeks. In Surabaya, the churches were filled to overflowing. God is moving in Southeast Asia.
I organized the new believers into evangelistic bands of three. Each band must go out and win three more. This is how the gospel will spread.
The churches of China are dead. They are filled with social programs and empty of the Holy Spirit. They need revival, and revival begins with repentance.
My body is wracked with disease. I have tuberculosis and intestinal parasites. But as long as I can stand, I will preach.
I wept as I read the story of the Cross during those 193 days. I was a sinner, a proud sinner, and God broke me in that asylum.
The trouble with the Chinese church is that it has Western form and no Chinese fire. We need fire — the fire of the Holy Spirit.
I am a small man with a loud voice. God made me this way so that all of China can hear the gospel.
Each sermon I preach costs me a piece of my life. I know I am burning out. But I would rather burn out than rust out.
My father was a pastor. He prayed for me before I was born. He named me 'Shangje' — 'Born of God.' I was destined for this work.
I preached in Hinghwa, my hometown, and thousands came. My own father wept. It was the greatest day of my life.
Do not tell me about your programs and your committees. Tell me: have you repented of your sins? Have you been washed in the blood?
I am dying at forty-three. I have preached for fifteen years. I have no regrets. I have fought the good fight, I have finished the course.
My wife has borne much. She raised our children alone while I traveled across China and Southeast Asia. She is the true saint.
The intellectuals in the church think they are too wise for the Cross. But the Cross is the wisdom of God, and all human wisdom is foolishness before it.
I have organized over two thousand evangelistic bands across China and Southeast Asia. These bands are the seed of revival. They will outlast me.
excerpts.source_title: Summary of ministry, compiled by associates
